name: Miniature Nut-Filled Pastries
recipeInstructions:
Place flour in a large bowl. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in butter or margarine until coarse crumbs are formed. Stir in egg yolks and sour cream until well mixed. Turn onto a floured surface; knead until smooth. Chill dough for 20 minutes.
Stir together nuts and corn syrup. Set aside. Divide dough into half. Roll out half of the dough to 1/8 inch thickness. Cut dough into 2-inch squares. Place 1/2 teaspoon nut filling diagonally across each square. Bring up unfilled corners of pastry to meet in the center over the filling. Pinch corners together firmly to secure. Place on a greased cookie sheet.
Bake in a preheated 400-degree oven for 12 minutes or until edges are lightly browned. Cool. Sprinkle with confectioners' sugar. Yield: Makes 8 dozen. Cookies store well.
